TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Creation Is Saved Successfully
TEST STEPS:
Login as Admin

Create new role

Configure permissions

Assign entities

Click Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role saved successfully

Success message displayed

Role appears in roles list

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Persists After Logout/Login
TEST STEPS:
Logout

Login again

Navigate to Roles
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role exists

All permissions remain unchanged

All entity assignments remain intact

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify No Data Loss on Role Edit After Re-Login
TEST STEPS:
Create role

Logout

Login

Edit role

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
No missing settings

No corrupted fields

Data updates correctly

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Creation Performance
TEST STEPS: Measure time from clicking Save until success response
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role saved within acceptable performance threshold

No noticeable delay

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify System Prevents Duplicate Role Name Within Same Tenant
TEST STEPS:
Create role named “Supervisor”

Attempt to create another role named “Supervisor”
EXPECTED RESULT:
System blocks creation

Clear validation message displayed

Role not created

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Name Is Case-Insensitive for Duplicate Check
TEST STEPS:
Create role named “Manager”

Attempt to create role named “manager”
EXPECTED RESULT:
System treats names as duplicate

Creation blocked

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Duplicate Role Name Across Different Tenants Is Allowed
EXPECTED RESULT: Same role name allowed in different tenants

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Permission Checks Execute Within Milliseconds
TEST STEPS:
Login as user with multiple permissions

Navigate between pages requiring permission validation

Measure API response time
EXPECTED RESULT:
Permission check response time within defined SLA (e.g., <100ms)

No visible UI lag

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Permission Check Under Load
Precondition: High number of users and roles
TEST STEPS: Simulate multiple concurrent permission checks
EXPECTED RESULT:
No degradation beyond acceptable threshold

No timeouts

No permission mismatch

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify No Permission Leakage
TEST STEPS:
Modify role permissions

Login as assigned user

Verify access matches updated permissions
EXPECTED RESULT:
Permissions enforced accurately

No cached stale permissions

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Editor Contains Entities and Privileges in Single Page
TEST STEPS:
Login as Admin

Click Create Role
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role name field visible

Entities tree visible

Privileges section visible

No navigation to separate pages required

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Can Be Created With Entities and Privileges Together
TEST STEPS:
Enter role name

Select entities

Select privileges

Click Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role saved successfully

Selected entities assigned

Selected privileges assigned

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Admin Can Clone Existing Role
TEST STEPS:
Select existing role

Click Clone

Modify role name

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
New role created

Original role unchanged

All entities and privileges copied correctly

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Modifying Cloned Role Does Not Affect Original
TEST STEPS:
Clone role

Remove privilege from cloned version

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Original role permissions remain intact

Changes apply only to cloned role

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Parent Selection Selects All Children
TEST STEPS: Select parent entity
EXPECTED RESULT: All child entities automatically selected

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Deselecting Parent Deselects All Children
TEST STEPS: Deselect parent entity
EXPECTED RESULT: All child entities are deselected

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: verify Partial Selection State
TEST STEPS: Select only one child entity
EXPECTED RESULT: Parent checkbox shows partial/indeterminate state

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Irrelevant Privileges Are Hidden Based on Selected Entities
TEST STEPS:
Select specific entity type

Observe privileges list
EXPECTED RESULT:
Only privileges relevant to selected entity are visible

Irrelevant privileges hidden automatically

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Privileges Update Dynamically When Entity Selection Changes
TEST STEPS:
Select Entity A

Observe privileges

Deselect Entity A

Select Entity B
EXPECTED RESULT:
Privileges update in real-time

No stale privileges remain visible

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Previously Selected Privilege Is Removed If It Becomes Irrelevant
TEST STEPS:
Select Entity A

Select related privilege

Change entity selection so privilege is no longer relevant
EXPECTED RESULT:
System blocks save with validation message

No invalid privilege-entity combination saved

TEST STEPS: Select 5 privileges
EXPECTED RESULT:
No lag

Selection completed smoothly within acceptable time

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Categories Organize Privileges Clearly
EXPECTED RESULT:
Privileges grouped logically

No duplicates across categories

Clear labeling

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Privilege Filtering With Multiple Entity Types Selected
TEST STEPS:

Select Entity B

Observe privileges
EXPECTED RESULT:
Combined relevant privileges appear

No duplication

No unrelated privilege leakage

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Rapid Entity Switching
TEST STEPS: Quickly select/deselect different entities multiple times
EXPECTED RESULT:
Privilege list updates correctly each time

No stale privileges remain

No JS errors

No flickering state corruption

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Save Blocked If Invalid Privilege-Entity Combination Exists
EXPECTED RESULT:
Save blocked OR

Admin modifies role
EXPECTED RESULT: Permissions updated dynamically

EXPECTED RESULT:
No duplicate data

No race condition

No system slowdown

EXPECTED RESULT:
Privileges filtered strictly per tenant

Login as Admin

Select role

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role assigned successfully

Role visible under user's assigned roles

EXPECTED RESULT:

Operation completes ≤ 5 seconds

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify All Group Members Receive Role

EXPECTED RESULT:

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Permission Merge Logic
EXPECTED RESULT:
Effective permissions show Read + Write

No duplicate privileges

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Permission Conflict Handling
EXPECTED RESULT:
System follows defined precedence rule

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Concurrent Role Assignment
EXPECTED RESULT:
No data corruption

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: verify Admin Can Deny Specific Privilege on Specific Entity
TEST STEPS:
Open existing role

Add override

Select entity

Deny specific privilege

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Override saved successfully

User with this role cannot access that privilege on that specific entity

Other entities remain unaffected

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Deny Override Takes Precedence Over Global Grant
TEST STEPS:
Add deny override for Entity A

Login as assigned user

Try to edit Entity A

Try to edit Entity B
EXPECTED RESULT:
Edit blocked on Entity A

Edit allowed on Entity B

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Admin Can Grant Privilege on Specific Entity Only
TEST STEPS:
Add grant override for Entity A

Save

Login as assigned user
EXPECTED RESULT:
Delete allowed on Entity A

Delete NOT allowed on other entities

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Admin Can View All Overrides in Table
TEST STEPS:
Open role

Navigate to Overrides section
EXPECTED RESULT:
Entity

Privilege

Type (Grant / Deny)

All overrides clearly listed

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Admin Can Remove Override
TEST STEPS:
Delete override from table

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Override removed

Role behavior reverts to global rule

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Adding Override Is Immediate
EXPECTED RESULT:
Override saved within few seconds

No delay

No need to recreate role

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Duplicate Override
TEST STEPS: Admin tries to add the same override (same role + entity + privilege) twice
EXPECTED RESULT: System prevents duplicate OR updates the existing override. No duplicate records created

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Override on Deleted/Invalid Entity
TEST STEPS: Admin attempts to create an override on a deleted or non-existing entity.
EXPECTED RESULT: Validation error. Override is not saved

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Remove Override
TEST STEPS: Admin deletes an existing override
EXPECTED RESULT: Access falls back to the global role definition. No stale permissions remain.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Large Number of Overrides
TEST STEPS: Role contains many overrides (e.g., 50+)
EXPECTED RESULT: Overrides table loads correctly without performance degradation.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Override Visibility & Audit
TEST STEPS: Admin views a role with overrides
EXPECTED RESULT: All overrides are clearly displayed and distinguishable from global permissions.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Performance Check
TEST STEPS: Admin adds or removes an override
EXPECTED RESULT: Action completes within seconds (no noticeable delay).

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Exclude Specific Privilege from User
TEST STEPS: Admin excludes one privilege from a user who belongs to a role
EXPECTED RESULT:
Privilege is removed immediately.

No new role is created.

Other users in the same role are not affected.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Restriction Speed
TEST STEPS: Admin applies restriction
EXPECTED RESULT:
Action completes within ~20 seconds.

No system delay.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Restore Excluded Privilege
TEST STEPS: Admin restores excluded privilege
EXPECTED RESULT:
Access restored with one click.

Restoration completes within ~10 seconds.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Audit Log Creation
TEST STEPS: Admin applies restriction
EXPECTED RESULT:
Admin name

Timestamp

Affected user

Excluded privilege

Reason

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Audit Log on Restore
TEST STEPS: Admin restores privilege
EXPECTED RESULT: Audit record logs restoration event with timestamp and actor.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Restricted User Experience
TEST STEPS: Restricted user tries to perform blocked action
EXPECTED RESULT:
Clear message explaining restriction.

Includes contact/reference info (if defined).

No generic error like “Access Denied”.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Exclude Privilege User Doesn’t Have
TEST STEPS: Admin tries to exclude a privilege not granted via role
EXPECTED RESULT: System prevents action OR shows validation message.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: 8. Multiple Restrictions on Same User
TEST STEPS: Admin excludes multiple privileges
EXPECTED RESULT:
All exclusions work independently.

Restoring one does not affect others.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: 9. User in Multiple Roles
TEST STEPS:
User belongs to 2 roles granting same privilege.
Admin excludes privilege at user level.
EXPECTED RESULT: User-level exclusion overrides all role permissions.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: 10. System Restart / Re-login
TEST STEPS: User logs out and back in after restriction
EXPECTED RESULT: Restriction persists.

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Exclude specific privilege from individual user
Precondition:
User is assigned to a role that includes Privilege X

Admin has permission to manage user overrides

Steps:
TEST STEPS:
Navigate to User Profile

Select "Exclude Privilege"

Choose Privilege X

Enter reason

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
Privilege X is excluded for that user only

Role definition remains unchanged

Audit log entry created (admin, timestamp, reason)
